metadata.name: Azure Virtual WAN
metadata.description: This pattern will create a Virtual WAN and optionally create Virtual Hubs, Azure Firewalls, and VPN/ExpressRoute Gateways.
Required. The name of the Virtual WAN.
Optional. The Azure region where the Virtual WAN will be created. Defaults to the resource group location if not specified.
Optional. Whether to allow branch-to-branch traffic within the Virtual WAN.
Optional. Lock settings for the Virtual WAN and associated resources.
Required. Whether to create a new P2S VPN server configuration.
Optional. Name of the P2S VPN server configuration.
Conditional. Entra ID audience for VPN authentication. Required if using Entra ID audience for VPN authentication.
Conditional. Entra ID issuer for VPN authentication. Required if using Entra ID authentication.
Conditional. Entra ID tenant for VPN authentication. Required if using Entra ID authentication.
Conditional. Configure user groups and IP Address Pools. Required if using Entra ID authentication.
Conditional. List of RADIUS client root certificates. Required if using RADIUS authentication.
Conditional. RADIUS server address. Required if using RADIUS authentication.
Conditional. List of RADIUS server root certificates. Required if using RADIUS authentication.
Conditional. RADIUS server secret. Required if using RADIUS authentication.
Conditional. List of RADIUS servers. Required if using RADIUS authentication.
Optional. VPN authentication types supported.
Optional. List of VPN client IPsec policies.
Optional. List of revoked VPN client certificates.
Optional. List of VPN client root certificates.
Optional. Supported VPN protocols.
Optional. Point-to-site VPN server configuration parameters for the Virtual WAN.
Optional. The name (as GUID) of the role assignment. If not provided, a GUID will be generated.

Required. The role to assign. You can provide either the display name of the role definition, the role definition GUID, or its fully qualified ID in the following format: '/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/roleDefinitions/c2f4ef07-c644-48eb-af81-4b1b4947fb11'.
Required. The principal ID of the principal (user/group/identity) to assign the role to.
Optional. The principal type of the assigned principal ID.
Optional. The description of the role assignment.

Optional. The conditions on the role assignment. This limits the resources it can be assigned to. e.g.: @Resource[Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/blobServices/containers:ContainerName] StringEqualsIgnoreCase "foo_storage_container".
Optional. Version of the condition.
Optional. The Resource Id of the delegated managed identity resource.
Optional. Role assignments to be applied to the Virtual WAN.
Optional. Tags to be applied to the Virtual WAN.
Optional. The type of Virtual WAN. Allowed values are Standard or Basic.
Required. The name of the Virtual Hub.
Required. The Azure region where the Virtual Hub will be created.
Required. The address prefix for the Virtual Hub.
Optional. Whether to allow branch-to-branch traffic within the Virtual Hub.
Optional. The routing preference for the Virtual Hub.
Optional. The route tables for the Virtual Hub.
Optional. The virtual network connections for the Virtual Hub.
Required. Whether to deploy a P2S VPN Gateway.
Conditional. Name of the connection configurations. Required if deployP2SVpnGateway is true.
Optional. Custom DNS servers for the P2S VPN Gateway.
Optional. Enable internet security for the P2S VPN Gateway.
Optional. Resource ID of the inbound route map.
Optional. Whether routing preference is internet.
Optional. Resource ID of the outbound route map.
Optional. Names of propagated labels.
Optional. Names of propagated route tables.
Optional. Static routes for VNet routes.
Conditional. Name of the VPN Gateway. Required if deployP2SVpnGateway is true.
Conditional. Address prefixes for the VPN client address pool. Required if deployP2SVpnGateway is true.
Optional. Scale unit for the VPN Gateway.
Optional. Associated route table for the VPN Gateway.
Optional. Point-to-site VPN parameters for the Virtual Hub.
Required. Whether to deploy a S2S VPN Gateway.
Conditional. Name of the VPN Gateway. Required if deployS2SVpnGateway is true.
Optional. Scale unit for the VPN Gateway.
Required. ASN for BGP. The default Azure VPN Gateway ASN is 65515. Custom ASN values require contacting Azure Support to enable ASN modification on the VPN Gateway.
Optional. BGP peering address.
Optional. IP configuration ID.
Optional. Custom BGP IP addresses.
Optional. BGP peering addresses.
Optional. Peer weight for BGP.
Optional. BGP settings for the VPN Gateway.
Optional. Enable BGP route translation for NAT.
Optional. Whether routing preference is internet.
Optional. Lock settings for the VPN Gateway.
Required. Address space for external mapping.
Conditional. External mappings for NAT rule. Required if defining NAT rules.
Required. Address space for internal mapping.
Conditional. Internal mappings for NAT rule. Required if defining NAT rules.
Conditional. Mode for NAT rule. Required if defining NAT rules.
Required. Name of the NAT rule.
Conditional. Type of NAT rule. Required if defining NAT rules.
Optional. IP configuration ID.
Optional. NAT rules for the VPN Gateway.
Required. Name of the VPN connection.
Optional. Connection bandwidth.
Optional. Enable BGP for the connection.
Optional. Enable internet security for the connection.
Optional. Resource ID of the remote VPN site.
Optional. Enable rate limiting.
Optional. Routing configuration for the connection.
Optional. Routing weight for the connection.
Optional. Shared key for the connection.
Optional. Use local Azure IP address.
Optional. Use policy-based traffic selectors.
Optional. VPN connection protocol type.
Optional. IPsec policies for the connection.
Optional. Traffic selector policies for the connection.
Optional. VPN link connections for the connection.
Optional. VPN connections for the VPN Gateway.
Optional. Site-to-site VPN parameters for the Virtual Hub.
Required. Whether to deploy an ExpressRoute Gateway.
Conditional. Name of the ExpressRoute Gateway. Required if deployExpressRouteGateway is true.
Optional. Allow non-Virtual WAN traffic.
Optional. Minimum bound for autoscale configuration.
Optional. Maximum bound for autoscale configuration.
Required. Name of the ExpressRoute connection.

Required. Resource ID of the ExpressRoute circuit peering (e.g., /subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.Network/expressRouteCircuits/{circuitName}/peerings/AzurePrivatePeering).
Required. Reference to the ExpressRoute circuit peering.
Optional. Authorization key for the connection.
Optional. Routing weight for the connection (0-32000).
Optional. Enable internet security for the connection.
Optional. Enable FastPath to vWan Firewall hub.
Optional. Enable private link fast path.
Optional. Routing configuration for the connection.
Required. Properties of the ExpressRoute connection.
Optional. ExpressRoute connections. Note: This array is passed directly to the ExpressRoute Gateway module and must match the Azure ARM API schema.
Optional. ExpressRoute parameters for the Virtual Hub.
Required. Whether to deploy a Secure Hub.
Optional. Resource ID of the firewall policy.
Conditional. Name of the Azure Firewall. Required if deploySecureHub is true.
Conditional. SKU for the Azure Firewall. Required if deploySecureHub is true.
Conditional. Number of public IPs for the Azure Firewall (1-100). Required if deploySecureHub is true.
Optional. Routing intent for the Azure Firewall.
Optional. The maximum number of capacity units for the Azure Firewall. Use null to reset to the service default.
Optional. The minimum number of capacity units for the Azure Firewall. Use null to reset to the service default.
Optional. The operation mode for Threat Intelligence.
Required. The name of the maintenance configuration assignment.
Required. The resource ID of the maintenance configuration to assign to the Azure Firewall.
Optional. The maintenance configuration to assign to the Azure Firewall.
Optional. Zone numbers e.g. 1,2,3.
Optional. Diagnostic settings for the Azure Firewall in the Secure Hub.
Optional. Secure Hub parameters for the Virtual Hub.
Optional. SKU for the Virtual Hub.
Optional. Tags to be applied to the Virtual Hub.
Optional. ASN for the Virtual Router.
Required. The minimum number of Routing Infrastructure Units for the Virtual Hub Router.
Optional. The autoscale configuration for the Virtual Router, defining the minimum number of Routing Infrastructure Units. Each unit supports ~1000 VMs and ~1 Gbps throughput (minimum 2).
Optional. IP addresses for the Virtual Router.
Optional. The name of the diagnostic setting.
Optional. Name of a Diagnostic Log category for a resource type this setting is applied to. Set the specific logs to collect here.
Optional. Name of a Diagnostic Log category group for a resource type this setting is applied to. Set to `allLogs` to collect all logs.
Optional. Enable or disable the category explicitly. Default is `true`.
Optional. The name of logs that will be streamed. "allLogs" includes all possible logs for the resource. Set to `[]` to disable log collection.
Required. Name of a Diagnostic Metric category for a resource type this setting is applied to. Set to `AllMetrics` to collect all metrics.
Optional. Enable or disable the category explicitly. Default is `true`.
Optional. The name of metrics that will be streamed. "allMetrics" includes all possible metrics for the resource. Set to `[]` to disable metric collection.
Optional. A string indicating whether the export to Log Analytics should use the default destination type, i.e. AzureDiagnostics, or use a destination type.

Optional. Resource ID of the diagnostic log analytics workspace. For security reasons, it is recommended to set diagnostic settings to send data to either storage account, log analytics workspace or event hub.

Optional. Resource ID of the diagnostic storage account. For security reasons, it is recommended to set diagnostic settings to send data to either storage account, log analytics workspace or event hub.
Optional. Resource ID of the diagnostic event hub authorization rule for the Event Hubs namespace in which the event hub should be created or streamed to.

Optional. Name of the diagnostic event hub within the namespace to which logs are streamed. Without this, an event hub is created for each log category. For security reasons, it is recommended to set diagnostic settings to send data to either storage account, log analytics workspace or event hub.
Optional. The full ARM resource ID of the Marketplace resource to which you would like to send Diagnostic Logs.
An AVM-aligned type for a diagnostic setting. To be used if both logs & metrics are supported by the resource provider.
Required. The route table name.
Optional. List of labels associated with this route table.
Required. The address prefix for the route.
Required. The destination type for the route.
Required. The name of the route.
Required. The next hop type for the route.
Required. The next hop IP address for the route.
Optional. List of all routes.
The type of a virtual hub route table.
Required. The connection name.
Optional. Enable internet security.
Required. Resource ID of the virtual network to link to.
Optional. Routing Configuration indicating the associated and propagated route tables for this connection.
The type of a hub virtual network connection.
Optional. Specify the name of lock.
Optional. Specify the type of lock.
Optional. Specify the notes of the lock.
An AVM-aligned type for a lock.
Optional. Configures Routing Intent to forward Private traffic to the firewall (RFC1918).
Optional. Configures Routing Intent to Forward Internet traffic to the firewall (0.0.0.0/0).
The type of a routing intent.
Optional. The address prefixes of the static route.
Optional. The name of the static route.
Optional. The next hop IP of the static route.
Optional. The static route configuration for the P2S VPN Gateway.
Optional. Determines whether the NVA in a SPOKE VNET is bypassed for traffic with destination in spoke.
Optional. The static route configuration for the P2S VPN Gateway.
Optional. A Type representing the VNET static routes for the P2S VPN Gateway.
Optional. The Diffie-Hellman group used in IKE phase 1. Required if using IKEv2.
Optional. The encryption algorithm used in IKE phase 1. Required if using IKEv2.
Optional. The integrity algorithm used in IKE phase 1. Required if using IKEv2.
Optional. The encryption algorithm used in IKE phase 2. Required if using IKEv2.
Optional. The integrity algorithm used in IKE phase 2. Required if using IKEv2.
Optional. The Perfect Forward Secrecy (PFS) group used in IKE phase 2. Required if using IKEv2.
Optional. The size of the SA data in kilobytes. Required if using IKEv2.
Optional. The lifetime of the SA in seconds. Required if using IKEv2.
Optional. Azure region where the Virtual WAN will be created.
Required. The parameters for the Virtual WAN.
Required. The parameters for the Virtual Hubs and associated networking components, required if configuring Virtual Hubs.
Optional. The lock settings for the Virtual WAN and associated components.
Optional. Enable/Disable usage telemetry for module.
Optional. Tags to be applied to all resources.

properties.routingPolicies: [concat(if(equals(tryGet(tryGet(tryGet(variables('hubConfigurations')[copyIndex()].hub, 'secureHubParameters'), 'routingIntent'), 'internetToFirewall'), true()), createArray(createObject('name', 'InternetTrafficPolicy', 'destinations', createArray('Internet'), 'nextHop', tryGet(if(variables('hubConfigurations')[variables('hubConfigurations')[copyIndex()].index].deploySecureHub, reference(format('firewallModule[{0}]', variables('hubConfigurations')[copyIndex()].index)), null()), 'outputs', 'resourceId', 'value'))), createArray()), if(equals(tryGet(tryGet(tryGet(variables('hubConfigurations')[copyIndex()].hub, 'secureHubParameters'), 'routingIntent'), 'privateToFirewall'), true()), createArray(createObject('name', 'PrivateTrafficPolicy', 'destinations', createArray('PrivateTraffic'), 'nextHop', tryGet(if(variables('hubConfigurations')[variables('hubConfigurations')[copyIndex()].index].deploySecureHub, reference(format('firewallModule[{0}]', variables('hubConfigurations')[copyIndex()].index)), null()), 'outputs', 'resourceId', 'value'))), createArray()))]

metadata.name: Virtual WANs
metadata.description: This module deploys a Virtual WAN.
Optional. Specify the name of lock.
Optional. Specify the type of lock.
Optional. Specify the notes of the lock.
An AVM-aligned type for a lock.
Optional. The name (as GUID) of the role assignment. If not provided, a GUID will be generated.

Required. The role to assign. You can provide either the display name of the role definition, the role definition GUID, or its fully qualified ID in the following format: '/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/roleDefinitions/c2f4ef07-c644-48eb-af81-4b1b4947fb11'.
Required. The principal ID of the principal (user/group/identity) to assign the role to.
Optional. The principal type of the assigned principal ID.
Optional. The description of the role assignment.

Optional. The conditions on the role assignment. This limits the resources it can be assigned to. e.g.: @Resource[Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/blobServices/containers:ContainerName] StringEqualsIgnoreCase "foo_storage_container".
Optional. Version of the condition.
Optional. The Resource Id of the delegated managed identity resource.
An AVM-aligned type for a role assignment.
Optional. Location where all resources will be created.
Required. Name of the Virtual WAN.
Optional. The type of the Virtual WAN.
Optional. True if branch to branch traffic is allowed.
Optional. Array of role assignments to create.
Optional. Tags of the resource.
Optional. Enable/Disable usage telemetry for module.
Optional. The lock settings of the service.

properties.notes: [coalesce(tryGet(parameters('lock'), 'notes'), if(equals(tryGet(parameters('lock'), 'kind'), 'CanNotDelete'), 'Cannot delete resource or child resources.', 'Cannot delete or modify the resource or child resources.'))]

metadata.description: The name of the virtual WAN.
The resource ID of the virtual WAN.
The resource group the virtual WAN was deployed into.
The location the resource was deployed into.

metadata.name: Virtual Hubs
metadata.description:
This module deploys a Virtual Hub.
If you are planning to deploy a Secure Virtual Hub (with an Azure Firewall integrated), please refer to the Azure Firewall module.
Required. The route table name.
Optional. List of labels associated with this route table.
Required. The address prefix for the route.
Required. The destination type for the route.
Required. The name of the route.
Required. The next hop type for the route.
Required. The next hop IP address for the route.
Optional. List of all routes.
The type of a virtual hub route table.
Optional. Configures Routing Intent to forward Private traffic to the firewall (RFC1918).
Optional. Configures Routing Intent to Forward Internet traffic to the firewall (0.0.0.0/0).
The type of a routing intent.
Required. The connection name.
Optional. Enable internet security.
Required. Resource ID of the virtual network to link to.
Optional. Routing Configuration indicating the associated and propagated route tables for this connection.
The type of a hub virtual network connection.
Optional. Specify the name of lock.
Optional. Specify the type of lock.
Optional. Specify the notes of the lock.
An AVM-aligned type for a lock.
Required. The virtual hub name.
Optional. Location for all resources.
Optional. Tags of the resource.
Required. Address-prefix for this VirtualHub.
Optional. Flag to control transit for VirtualRouter hub.
Optional. Resource ID of the Azure Firewall to link to.
Optional. Resource ID of the Express Route Gateway to link to.
Optional. Resource ID of the Point-to-Site VPN Gateway to link to.
Optional. The preferred routing preference for this virtual hub.
Optional. The preferred routing gateway types.
Optional. The VirtualHub route tables.
Optional. ID of the Security Partner Provider to link to.
Optional. The Security Provider name.
Optional. The sku of this VirtualHub.
Optional. List of all virtual hub route table v2s associated with this VirtualHub.
Optional. VirtualRouter ASN.
Optional. VirtualRouter IPs.
Required. The minimum number of virtual routers in the scale set.
Optional. The auto scale configuration for the virtual router.
Required. Resource ID of the virtual WAN to link to.
Optional. Resource ID of the VPN Gateway to link to.
Optional. The routing intent configuration to create for the virtual hub.
Optional. Route tables to create for the virtual hub.
Optional. Virtual network connections to create for the virtual hub.
Optional. The lock settings of the service.
Optional. Enable/Disable usage telemetry for module.

properties.notes: [coalesce(tryGet(parameters('lock'), 'notes'), if(equals(tryGet(parameters('lock'), 'kind'), 'CanNotDelete'), 'Cannot delete resource or child resources.', 'Cannot delete or modify the resource or child resources.'))]

metadata.name: Virtual Hub Routing Intent
metadata.description: This module configures Routing Intent for a Virtual Hub; this module requires an existing Virtual Hub, as well the firewall Resource ID.
Required. Hub firewall Resource ID.
Optional. The name of the routing intent configuration.
Required. Name of the Virtual Hub.
Optional. Configures Routing Intent to forward Private traffic to the firewall (RFC1918).
Optional. Configures Routing Intent to Forward Internet traffic to the firewall (0.0.0.0/0).

properties.routingPolicies: [if(and(equals(parameters('internetToFirewall'), true()), equals(parameters('privateToFirewall'), true())), createArray(createObject('name', '_policy_PublicTraffic', 'destinations', createArray('Internet'), 'nextHop', parameters('azureFirewallResourceId')), createObject('name', '_policy_PrivateTraffic', 'destinations', createArray('PrivateTraffic'), 'nextHop', parameters('azureFirewallResourceId'))), if(and(equals(parameters('internetToFirewall'), true()), equals(parameters('privateToFirewall'), false())), createArray(createObject('name', '_policy_PublicTraffic', 'destinations', createArray('Internet'), 'nextHop', parameters('azureFirewallResourceId'))), if(and(equals(parameters('internetToFirewall'), false()), equals(parameters('privateToFirewall'), true())), createArray(createObject('name', '_policy_PrivateTraffic', 'destinations', createArray('PrivateTraffic'), 'nextHop', parameters('azureFirewallResourceId'))), null())))]

metadata.description: The name of the Routing Intent configuration.
The resource ID of the Routing Intent configuration.
The resource group the Routing Intent configuration was deployed into.

metadata.name: Virtual Hub Route Tables
metadata.description: This module deploys a Virtual Hub Route Table.
Required. The route table name.
Conditional. The name of the parent virtual hub. Required if the template is used in a standalone deployment.
Optional. List of labels associated with this route table.
Optional. List of all routes.
The name of the deployed virtual hub route table.
The resource ID of the deployed virtual hub route table.
The resource group the virtual hub route table was deployed into.

metadata.name: Virtual Hub Virtual Network Connections
metadata.description: This module deploys a Virtual Hub Virtual Network Connection.
Required. The connection name.
Conditional. The name of the parent virtual hub. Required if the template is used in a standalone deployment.
Optional. Enable internet security.
Required. Resource ID of the virtual network to link to.
Optional. Routing Configuration indicating the associated and propagated route tables for this connection.
The resource group the virtual hub connection was deployed into.
The resource ID of the virtual hub connection.
The name of the virtual hub connection.
The resource group the virtual hub was deployed into.
The resource ID of the virtual hub.
The name of the virtual hub.
The location the resource was deployed into.

metadata.name: Azure Firewalls
metadata.description: This module deploys an Azure Firewall.
Required. The name of the maintenance configuration assignment.
Required. The resource ID of the maintenance configuration to assign to the Azure Firewall.
The type of a maintenance configuration.
Optional. The name of the diagnostic setting.
Optional. Name of a Diagnostic Log category for a resource type this setting is applied to. Set the specific logs to collect here.
Optional. Name of a Diagnostic Log category group for a resource type this setting is applied to. Set to `allLogs` to collect all logs.
Optional. Enable or disable the category explicitly. Default is `true`.
Optional. The name of logs that will be streamed. "allLogs" includes all possible logs for the resource. Set to `[]` to disable log collection.
Required. Name of a Diagnostic Metric category for a resource type this setting is applied to. Set to `AllMetrics` to collect all metrics.
Optional. Enable or disable the category explicitly. Default is `true`.
Optional. The name of metrics that will be streamed. "allMetrics" includes all possible metrics for the resource. Set to `[]` to disable metric collection.
Optional. A string indicating whether the export to Log Analytics should use the default destination type, i.e. AzureDiagnostics, or use a destination type.

Optional. Resource ID of the diagnostic log analytics workspace. For security reasons, it is recommended to set diagnostic settings to send data to either storage account, log analytics workspace or event hub.

Optional. Resource ID of the diagnostic storage account. For security reasons, it is recommended to set diagnostic settings to send data to either storage account, log analytics workspace or event hub.
Optional. Resource ID of the diagnostic event hub authorization rule for the Event Hubs namespace in which the event hub should be created or streamed to.

Optional. Name of the diagnostic event hub within the namespace to which logs are streamed. Without this, an event hub is created for each log category. For security reasons, it is recommended to set diagnostic settings to send data to either storage account, log analytics workspace or event hub.
Optional. The full ARM resource ID of the Marketplace resource to which you would like to send Diagnostic Logs.
An AVM-aligned type for a diagnostic setting. To be used if both logs & metrics are supported by the resource provider.
Optional. Specify the name of lock.
Optional. Specify the type of lock.
Optional. Specify the notes of the lock.
An AVM-aligned type for a lock.
Optional. The name (as GUID) of the role assignment. If not provided, a GUID will be generated.

Required. The role to assign. You can provide either the display name of the role definition, the role definition GUID, or its fully qualified ID in the following format: '/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/roleDefinitions/c2f4ef07-c644-48eb-af81-4b1b4947fb11'.
Required. The principal ID of the principal (user/group/identity) to assign the role to.
Optional. The principal type of the assigned principal ID.
Optional. The description of the role assignment.

Optional. The conditions on the role assignment. This limits the resources it can be assigned to. e.g.: @Resource[Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/blobServices/containers:ContainerName] StringEqualsIgnoreCase "foo_storage_container".
Optional. Version of the condition.
Optional. The Resource Id of the delegated managed identity resource.
An AVM-aligned type for a role assignment.
Required. Name of the Azure Firewall.
Optional. Tier of an Azure Firewall.

Conditional. Shared services Virtual Network resource ID. The virtual network ID containing AzureFirewallSubnet. If a Public IP is not provided, then the Public IP that is created as part of this module will be applied with the subnet provided in this variable. Required if `virtualHubId` is empty.
Optional. The Public IP resource ID to associate to the Azure Firewall. If empty, then the Public IP that is created as part of this module will be applied to the Azure Firewall.
Optional. This is to add any additional Public IP configurations on top of the Public IP with subnet IP configuration.
Optional. Specifies the properties of the Public IP to create and be used by the Firewall, if no existing public IP was provided.

Optional. The Management Public IP resource ID to associate to the AzureFirewallManagementSubnet. If empty, then the Management Public IP that is created as part of this module will be applied to the AzureFirewallManagementSubnet.

Optional. Specifies the properties of the Management Public IP to create and be used by Azure Firewall. If it's not provided and managementIPResourceID is empty, a '-mip' suffix will be appended to the Firewall's name.
Optional. Collection of application rule collections used by Azure Firewall.
Optional. Collection of network rule collections used by Azure Firewall.
Optional. Collection of NAT rule collections used by Azure Firewall.
Optional. Resource ID of the Firewall Policy that should be attached.
Conditional. IP addresses associated with AzureFirewall. Required if `virtualHubId` is supplied & `publicIPResourceID` is empty.
Conditional. The virtualHub resource ID to which the firewall belongs. Required if `virtualNetworkId` is empty.
Optional. The operation mode for Threat Intel.
Optional. The maximum number of capacity units for this azure firewall. Use null to reset the value to the service default.
Optional. The minimum number of capacity units for this azure firewall. Use null to reset the value to the service default.
Optional. The list of Availability zones to use for the zone-redundant resources. Pass an empty array to disable zone-redundancy for regions that do not support availability zones.
Optional. Enable/Disable to support Forced Tunneling and Packet capture scenarios.
Optional. The diagnostic settings of the service.
Optional. Location for all resources.
Optional. The lock settings of the service.
Optional. Array of role assignments to create.
Optional. Tags of the Azure Firewall resource.
Optional. The additional properties used to further config this azure firewall. Used for DNS proxy configuration (e.g., `Network.DNS.EnableProxy`).
Optional. The extended location of type local virtual network gateway.
Optional. The maintenance configuration to assign to the Azure Firewall.
Optional. Enable/Disable usage telemetry for module.

metadata.name: Public IP Addresses
metadata.description: This module deploys a Public IP Address.
Optional. The name of the diagnostic setting.
Optional. Name of a Diagnostic Log category for a resource type this setting is applied to. Set the specific logs to collect here.
Optional. Name of a Diagnostic Log category group for a resource type this setting is applied to. Set to `allLogs` to collect all logs.
Optional. Enable or disable the category explicitly. Default is `true`.
Optional. The name of logs that will be streamed. "allLogs" includes all possible logs for the resource. Set to `[]` to disable log collection.
Required. Name of a Diagnostic Metric category for a resource type this setting is applied to. Set to `AllMetrics` to collect all metrics.
Optional. Enable or disable the category explicitly. Default is `true`.
Optional. The name of metrics that will be streamed. "allMetrics" includes all possible metrics for the resource. Set to `[]` to disable metric collection.
Optional. A string indicating whether the export to Log Analytics should use the default destination type, i.e. AzureDiagnostics, or use a destination type.

Optional. Resource ID of the diagnostic log analytics workspace. For security reasons, it is recommended to set diagnostic settings to send data to either storage account, log analytics workspace or event hub.

Optional. Resource ID of the diagnostic storage account. For security reasons, it is recommended to set diagnostic settings to send data to either storage account, log analytics workspace or event hub.
Optional. Resource ID of the diagnostic event hub authorization rule for the Event Hubs namespace in which the event hub should be created or streamed to.

Optional. Name of the diagnostic event hub within the namespace to which logs are streamed. Without this, an event hub is created for each log category. For security reasons, it is recommended to set diagnostic settings to send data to either storage account, log analytics workspace or event hub.
Optional. The full ARM resource ID of the Marketplace resource to which you would like to send Diagnostic Logs.
An AVM-aligned type for a diagnostic setting. To be used if both logs & metrics are supported by the resource provider.
Optional. Specify the name of lock.
Optional. Specify the type of lock.
Optional. Specify the notes of the lock.
An AVM-aligned type for a lock.
Optional. The name (as GUID) of the role assignment. If not provided, a GUID will be generated.

Required. The role to assign. You can provide either the display name of the role definition, the role definition GUID, or its fully qualified ID in the following format: '/providers/Microsoft.Authorization/roleDefinitions/c2f4ef07-c644-48eb-af81-4b1b4947fb11'.
Required. The principal ID of the principal (user/group/identity) to assign the role to.
Optional. The principal type of the assigned principal ID.
Optional. The description of the role assignment.

Optional. The conditions on the role assignment. This limits the resources it can be assigned to. e.g.: @Resource[Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/blobServices/containers:ContainerName] StringEqualsIgnoreCase "foo_storage_container".
Optional. Version of the condition.
Optional. The Resource Id of the delegated managed identity resource.
An AVM-aligned type for a role assignment.
Required. The name of the Public IP Address.
Optional. Resource ID of the Public IP Prefix object. This is only needed if you want your Public IPs created in a PIP Prefix.
Optional. The public IP address allocation method.
Optional. A list of availability zones denoting the IP allocated for the resource needs to come from.
Optional. IP address version.
Optional. The DNS settings of the public IP address.
Optional. The list of tags associated with the public IP address.
Optional. The lock settings of the service.
Optional. Name of a public IP address SKU.
Optional. Tier of a public IP address SKU.
Optional. The DDoS protection plan configuration associated with the public IP address.
Optional. The delete option for the public IP address.
Optional. Location for all resources.
Optional. Array of role assignments to create.
Optional. Enable/Disable usage telemetry for module.
Optional. The idle timeout of the public IP address.
Optional. Tags of the resource.
Optional. The diagnostic settings of the service.

Conditional. The audience for the AAD/Entra authentication. Required if configuring Entra ID authentication.
Optional. The P2S configuration policy groups for the configuration.
Optional. The revoked RADIUS client certificates for the configuration.

Optional. The root certificates of the RADIUS server.

Optional. Connection bandwidth in MBPS.

Optional. Routing weight.

metadata.name: VPN Gateway NAT Rules
metadata.description: This module deploys a VPN Gateway NAT Rule.
Required. The name of the NAT rule.
Conditional. The name of the parent VPN gateway this NAT rule is associated with. Required if the template is used in a standalone deployment.
Optional. An address prefix range of destination IPs on the outside network that source IPs will be mapped to. In other words, your post-NAT address prefix range.
Optional. An address prefix range of source IPs on the inside network that will be mapped to a set of external IPs. In other words, your pre-NAT address prefix range.
Optional. A NAT rule must be configured to a specific VPN Gateway instance. This is applicable to Dynamic NAT only. Static NAT rules are automatically applied to both VPN Gateway instances.

Optional. The type of NAT rule for VPN NAT. IngressSnat mode (also known as Ingress Source NAT) is applicable to traffic entering the Azure hub's site-to-site VPN gateway. EgressSnat mode (also known as Egress Source NAT) is applicable to traffic leaving the Azure hub's Site-to-site VPN gateway.

Optional. The type of NAT rule for VPN NAT. Static one-to-one NAT establishes a one-to-one relationship between an internal address and an external address while Dynamic NAT assigns an IP and port based on availability.
The name of the NAT rule.
The resource ID of the NAT rule.
The name of the resource group the NAT rule was deployed into.

Optional. The traffic selector policies to be considered by this connection.

Optional. Use local Azure IP to initiate connection.
Optional. Enable rate limiting.
Optional. Enable internet security.
Optional. Enable BGP flag.
Optional. Routing weight for VPN connection.
Optional. Expected bandwidth in MBPS. This parameter is deprecated and should be avoided in favor of VpnSiteLinkConnection configuration.
Optional. Gateway connection protocol.
Optional. SharedKey for the VPN connection.
Optional. Reference to a VPN site to link to.
The name of the VPN connection.
The resource ID of the VPN connection.
The name of the resource group the VPN connection was deployed into.
The name of the VPN gateway.
The resource ID of the VPN gateway.
The name of the resource group the VPN gateway was deployed into.
The location the resource was deployed into.
The resource IDs of the NAT rules.
The resource IDs of the VPN connections.
